content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks for LLMs that rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback can be enhanced by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives central to LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical self-refinement system that outperforms both original approaches. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data while fixing its key limitation of repeating the same blind spots inherited from the initial output.
